text: The surgeon identifies filmy adhesions in the surgical field and locates Rouvier's sulcus behind them. The sulcus is visible to the left, serving as a landmark for the dissection of Calot's triangle.

The surgeon identifies and dissects the cystic artery and cystic duct, achieving the critical view of safety. Immunofluorescence imaging is used intermittently to enhance visualization. The cystic duct is clearly seen entering the gallbladder, with the liver visible in the background.
The surgeon places two clips on the stay side and one clip on the specimen side of the cystic duct. The cystic plate appears average in width, thinning towards the top.
